For Educators
Bilingual call & response song

Many Ways Forward
Muchas Formas de Avanzar

An upbeat, joyful Cuban / Caribbean big-band style song about flexible participation, different rhythms, and belonging in many ways.

▶
Many Ways Forward / Muchas Formas de Avanzar
3:18
English Español Side by Side
✦
Many Ways Forward Multiple pathways, flexible rhythms, and shared movement.
Upload file as audio/many-ways-forward.mp3 to activate this player.
How to use this song

Call, response, movement, and choice

Leader / All parts make it easy for everyone to join in their own way: singing, echoing, clapping, moving, listening, or stepping in quietly.

Teacher note

Participation does not have to look the same to count.

This song supports classrooms where flexible participation, multilingual expression, and different rhythms are recognized as strengths.

English
Intro

Hey—
not one way.

Many ways.

Verse 1

Some start fast—
some wait.

Some jump in—
some hesitate.

Some talk it out—
some move first.

Some need quiet
before the words.

Pre-Chorus – lift

No one path—
no single line,

Different rhythms—
same shared time.

Chorus

Leader:
Many ways forward!

All:
Many ways!

Leader:
Not just one road—

All:
Not just one way!

All together

Step in, step through,
however you do—

Many ways forward,
and they all count too.

Verse 2

Some build slow—
some spark quick.

Some stay steady—
some shift.

Some get there
by going around,

Some find their way
without a sound.

Pre-Chorus

Not the same—
and that’s the point,

Different paths—
but still joined.

Chorus

Leader:
Many ways forward!

All:
Many ways!

Leader:
Come as you are—

All:
You’re already on the way!

All together

Step in, step through,
however you do—

Many ways forward,
and they all count too.

Bridge

Take your time.
Find your pace.

There’s no rush—
in this space.

You don’t have to
match the sound—

Just step in.
You’re already found.

Final Chorus

Leader:
Many ways forward!

All:
Many ways!

Leader:
Side by side—

All:
Different ways!

All together

Step in, step through,
however you do—

Many ways forward,
and they all count too.

Outro

Come in how you are—

We’ll go from there.

Español
Intro

Ey—
no hay una sola forma.

Hay muchas.

Verso 1

Algunos van rápido—
otros esperan.

Algunos se lanzan—
otros lo piensan.

Algunos hablan—
otros actúan.

Algunos necesitan calma
antes de la voz.

Pre-Coro

No hay un camino—
ni una sola línea,

Ritmos distintos—
mismo tiempo compartido.

Coro

Líder:
¡Muchas formas de avanzar!

Todos:
¡Muchas formas!

Líder:
No es un solo camino—

Todos:
¡No es una sola forma!

Todos juntos

Da un paso, sigue adelante,
a tu manera—

Muchas formas de avanzar,
y todas cuentan también.

Verso 2

Algunos construyen lento—
otros encienden rápido.

Algunos siguen firmes—
otros cambian.

Algunos llegan
dando la vuelta,

Otros encuentran su rumbo
sin hacer ruido.

Pre-Coro

No es igual—
y de eso se trata,

Caminos distintos—
pero juntos avanzan.

Coro

Líder:
¡Muchas formas de avanzar!

Todos:
¡Muchas formas!

Líder:
Ven como eres—

Todos:
¡Ya estás en camino!

Todos juntos

Da un paso, sigue adelante,
a tu manera—

Muchas formas de avanzar,
y todas cuentan también.

Puente

Tómate tu tiempo.
Encuentra tu paso.

No hay prisa
en este espacio.

No tienes que
sonar igual—

Solo entra.
Ya perteneces.

Coro Final

Líder:
¡Muchas formas de avanzar!

Todos:
¡Muchas formas!

Líder:
Lado a lado—

Todos:
¡Formas distintas!

Todos juntos

Da un paso, sigue adelante,
a tu manera—

Muchas formas de avanzar,
y todas cuentan también.

Outro

Llega como eres—

Seguimos desde ahí.

👏

Call & response

Use Leader / All parts to make participation simple and joyful.

🪘

Rhythm choices

Students can clap, tap, move, sing, echo, listen, or join later.

🌊

Many pathways

Use the song before group work, circles, partner talk, or flexible learning stations.

First week pathway

Five gentle ways to enter

1

Listen

Notice the different ways people join.

2

Echo

Practice Leader / All call and response.

3

Move

Create gestures for “step in” and “many ways.”

4

Reflect

Complete: “I participate best when…”

5

Celebrate

Name how different ways helped the group.

Pathway
